title: The Painting That Set Me Free

story.opening: <break time="400ms"/>The painting that saved my creative life <break time="350ms"/>was the worst painting I ever made.
story.body: <break time="350ms"/>When I came back after three years, <break time="300ms"/>I was terrified to make anything. <break time="250ms"/>The pressure was enormous. <break time="500ms"/>So I made a deal with myself: <break time="400ms"/>I would make the worst painting I could. <break time="350ms"/>On purpose. <break time="300ms"/>Intentionally terrible. <break time="500ms"/>I used colors that clashed. <break time="400ms"/>Drew shapes that were ugly. <break time="350ms"/>Made marks without any skill. <break time="300ms"/>And when I was done - <break time="250ms"/>I felt free. <break time="500ms"/>Because the worst had happened. <break time="400ms"/>I made something bad. <break time="350ms"/>And nothing exploded. <break time="300ms"/>I was still here. <break time="250ms"/>Still a person who makes things.
story.reflection: <break time="500ms"/>Give yourself permission to make terrible work. <break time="400ms"/>Not accidentally terrible. <break time="350ms"/>Intentionally terrible. <break time="300ms"/>It breaks the perfectionism spell. <break time="500ms"/>Once you've made the worst thing - <break time="400ms"/>everything else is easier.

when_to_share: When someone is paralyzed by perfectionism or fear of making bad work
